raw_consumer_statement:
I disputed a {$1700.00} charge with American Express for a XXXX XXXX purchase because the product was misrepresented. The item was sold as a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, but the delivered product has no connectors or hardware and can not function as a modular sectional. The pieces separate under normal sitting and can not be used as one unit. 

I submitted merchant marketing materials showing the product labeled as modular, photos of the underside showing no connectors, and photos showing the separation during normal use. I clearly stated the dispute reason as not as described / misrepresentation. 

American Express twice denied the dispute without addressing this claim. Instead, both denial letters focus on unrelated delivery issues, store credits, and color/configuration mix-ups from XX/XX/year>. Those facts are not the basis of my dispute and do not resolve whether the product functions as advertised. 

I requested reconsideration and provided additional documentation, but American Express again ruled solely on the merchants delivery and return-policy narrative rather than the misrepresentation issue raised. Both denials included the same merchant bookkeeping documents, which do not address whether the product functions as advertised. I am submitting this complaint because American Express failed to evaluate the dispute reason I raised despite multiple submissions of evidence.
